arts.ttl: Fiorentina vs. Lazio: time to restart
arts.sum: Fiorentina (60.5%) and Lazio (54.8%) are the two teams with the highest percentage of shots on target in this Serie A season; furthermore, only Viola (26) have hit the target more often than the Biancocelesti (23) in the current championship (on par with Milan and Atalanta). Fiorentina are the team against which Lazio have won the most matches (59) and scored the most goals (209) in Serie A –the 150 previous meetings between the two teams is completed by 48 Viola wins and 43 draws.

arts.ttl: Felipe Anderson Admits: ”Never Thought I’d Leave Lazio”
arts.sum: Former Lazio winger Felipe Anderson was expecting to end his playing career in the Italian capital, at least before the opportunity to return home presented itself. The 31-year-old had two spells with the Biancocelesti, the first as a young and aspiring winger which dates all the way back to 2013. arts.ttl: Lazio New Stadium Project Taking Shape – Costs & Capacity Revealed
arts.sum: Lazio are reportedly making concrete steps in their plans to own and renovate the Stadio Flaminio. Club president Claudio Lotito has been tirelessly working on this front for quite some time, and as Il Messaggero (via La Lazio Siamo Noi) reports, the project is finally beginning to take shape. arts.ttl: Fiorentina vs Lazio | Serie A | Team News & Probable Lineups
arts.sum: On Sunday’s early Serie A kickoff, Fiorentina will host Lazio at Stadio Artemio Franchi in Florence. The Viola are still searching for their first victory of the season, while the Biancocelesti will be looking to build on last weekend’s win over Hellas Verona and start forging a winning streak. arts.ttl: Serie A | Fiorentina v Lazio: Match Preview
arts.sum: Fiorentina will be aiming to secure their first Serie A victory of the season when they host Lazio at the Stadio Artemio Franchi on Sunday afternoon. The Viola have struggled to find form in the early stages of the campaign, while Lazio look to build on their solid start under new manager Marco Baroni. Fiorentina enter this match winless in their opening four Serie A fixtures, marking their worst start to a league campaign since 2019. Raffaele Palladino’s side have drawn three and lost one so far, most recently falling to a 3-2 defeat against Atalanta last weekend. arts.ttl: Baroni Reveals Who Could Replace Castellanos in Fiorentina vs Lazio, Disagrees With Sarri Over Rotation
arts.sum: Lazio coach Marco Baroni names two players who could replace the injured Taty Castellanos against Fiorentina. The Biancocelesti will lock horns with the Viola on Sunday’s early Serie A kickoff. In his pre-match press conference (via La Lazio Siamo Noi), Baroni confirmed the absence of both Castellanos and Samuel Gigot due to injuries. arts.ttl: Lazio Confirm Gigot Suffered Shoulder Injury
arts.sum: Samuel Gigot will have to wait a bit longer to make his Lazio debut after picking up a shoulder injury during training. The 30-year-old completed a deadline-day switch from Olympique Marseille to the Biancocelesti, replacing Nicolo Casale who joined Bologna on loan with an obligation to buy.
